Breezy southwest winds 15- 25 mph with gusts to 35 mph amid relative humidity of 5 -15% will continue across much of the Southwest to the southern High Plains today creating elevated to critical conditions. A cold front will move through the Great Basin with  breezy and dry southwest winds ahead of it, and northwest winds behind the front. North winds 10- 25 mph with gusts 25- 40 mph will develop today and tonight across much of the California Central Valley amid relative humidity of 10- 20% and poor overnight recovery creating elevated to critical conditions. Scattered showers are expected in the Northwest, mainly west of the Cascades, with a few showers into the northern Rockies, as well. Westerly winds 10- 20 mph with gusts to 30 mph are expected across the Upper Great Lakes today amid relative humidity of 15- 25% creating elevated conditions. Persistently low afternoon relative humidity below 30% will continue in most of the Piedmont to coastal plain of the Southeast, with isolated afternoon thunderstorms expected to develop across the northern Gulf Coast into Georgia and Florida. Dry conditions and near normal temperatures are expected across the Alaska Interior today, with locally breezy downslope winds developing off the Alaska Range.   National Weather Service, Storm Prediction Center : Fire Weather Outlook Map  and discussion National Weather Service:  Fire Weather Hazards Map  National Weather Service, Weather Prediction Center : National Forecast Chart  and  Short Range Forecast Discussion  National Predictive Services Outlook  

6 Minutes for Safety topics can be found here.
